early_blight.description: Causes dark concentric rings on older leaves. Thrives in warm, humid conditions and spreads via rain splash.

treatmentWindows.beforeRain: Apply fungicide at least 12 hours before expected rain
treatmentWindows.afterRain: Reapply copper-based fungicide 24 hours after rain clears
treatmentWindows.highHumidity: Increase application frequency when humidity stays above 75%

late_blight.description: Highly destructive water mold. Dark water-soaked lesions; spreads rapidly in cool, wet weather.

treatmentWindows.beforeRain: Apply preventive fungicide (mancozeb or chlorothalonil) before rain
treatmentWindows.afterRain: Inspect all plants immediately after rain; remove infected tissue
treatmentWindows.highHumidity: Apply systemic fungicide every 5 days during prolonged humid spells

leaf_mold.description: Yellow patches on leaf surfaces; olive-grey mold below. Primarily a greenhouse/enclosed terrace problem.

treatmentWindows.beforeRain: No specific rain-triggered timing needed; focus on ventilation
treatmentWindows.afterRain: Prune lower leaves to improve airflow after wet spells
treatmentWindows.highHumidity: Improve air circulation; avoid overhead watering when humidity exceeds 85%

powdery_mildew.description: White dusty coating on leaves. Unlike most fungi, prefers dry conditions with moderate humidity.

treatmentWindows.beforeRain: Rain can wash spores; apply neem oil before forecasted rain for extra protection
treatmentWindows.afterRain: Monitor for rebound; apply sulfur-based fungicide 48 hours after rain
treatmentWindows.highHumidity: Powdery mildew tolerates low humidity; focus on wind spread management

bacterial_spot.description: Small water-soaked lesions that turn dark brown. Spreads rapidly in rainy, warm, windy conditions.

treatmentWindows.beforeRain: Apply copper bactericide 24 hours before expected rain
treatmentWindows.afterRain: Avoid working among wet plants; reapply copper after rain subsides
treatmentWindows.highHumidity: Apply weekly copper sprays during prolonged wet periods

anthracnose.description: Dark sunken lesions on fruits and leaves. Heavy rains spread spores rapidly.

treatmentWindows.beforeRain: Apply mancozeb before monsoon onset and before each heavy rain
treatmentWindows.afterRain: Remove and dispose of fallen infected material after rain
treatmentWindows.highHumidity: Increase spray frequency to every 7 days during high humidity
